events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a vanity drain that drops vertically into a P-trap, then immediately turns downward through the cabinet floor with no horizontal trap arm before the vent connection. This is essentially an S-trap configuration, which is prohibited by both IPC and UPC. The gurgling when the tub drains indicates trap seal loss due to siphonage.

Key issues:
1. S-trap configuration - prohibited by code (IPC 1002.3, UPC 1002.1)
2. No horizontal trap arm before vent connection
3. No visible cleanout
4. Gurgling indicates siphonage/trap seal depletion

Decision: fail
Risk: medium (sewer gas entry, unsanitary)
Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective

    "Vanity drain is configured as an S-trap: vertical drop from P-trap directly downward with no horizontal trap arm before the vent",
    "No vent connection on a horizontal trap arm; trap is unvented/vented improperly",
    "No visible cleanout on the trap or drain line",
    "Gurgling indicates trap seal is being siphoned by fixture drainage on the shared branch"

  "actions": [
    "Reconfigure drain to a proper P-trap with a horizontal trap arm sized per code before the vent takeoff",
    "Install a vent on the horizontal trap arm or convert to an AAV where permitted by code",
    "Add a cleanout per IPC/UPC requirements",
    "Verify trap seal retention after correction by running fixtures and observing for gurgling/siphonage"
  ],
  "rationale": "The described configuration is an S-trap, which is prohibited by both IPC and UPC because the vertical drop after the trap causes siphonage of the trap seal. The gurgling confirms trap seal loss from negative pressure when the tub drains. S-traps have been disallowed for decades; a compliant installation requires a horizontal trap arm with a vent connection before any vertical drop.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 1002.3 - traps shall not be S-traps",
    "IPC 1002.1 - fixture traps required and shall be self-cleaning",
    "IPC 903.1 / UPC 901.2 - venting required to protect trap seals",
    "IPC 318.1 / UPC 707 - cleanout requirements",
    "UPC 1002.3 - S-traps prohibited"
  ]
}
